steps.title: Use the equilateral triangle height
steps.content: Find the side length from the perimeter first. Then use the equilateral-triangle height formula $h=\frac{s\sqrt{3}}{2}$.

steps.title: Find the side length from the perimeter
steps.content:
The perimeter of an equilateral triangle equals three times the side length. With a perimeter of 624 cm:
$$\text{Side length} = \frac{624}{3} = 208 \text{ cm}$$

steps.title: Apply the equilateral triangle height formula
steps.content:
For an equilateral triangle with side length $s$, use the height formula. Substituting $s = 208$:
$$h = \frac{208\sqrt{3}}{2} = 104\sqrt{3} \text{ cm}$$
Since the height is expressed as $k\sqrt{3}$ centimeters, we have $k = 104$.
